Off Calendar Apr-24-2009 Advanced From May-13-09 To May-11-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Interpleader Action
Off Calendar May-21-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Matter heard with case #486199
Appearances: Richard Canatella for and with Lillian Najarro. Victoria Sood for Adella Najarro. Michelle McIsaac Esq. on behalf of Hartford Life. Michael Valencia for Donald Dowling.
The Court inquired of the Annuity status, Probate Court to assert primary jurisdiction over the matter and who is Maria Blanco. Ms. Sood stated that pending the Spousal Property Petition, matter should remain in the Probate Court and that Mr. Dowling had spoke with Mr. Canatella about mediation. Ms. McIssac stated, since the estate is not making claim to benefits and the trust is not part of the estate. If the trust is seeking part of benefits, then parties is entitled under the law to bring it to Civil Court, but will agreed to mediation. Mr. Canatella argued that the Interpleader Petition is in probate and the Will Contest is identical and involved the same parties. The Court stated that if parties agreed to mediation, the Court will take the matter under submission.
Pending the hearing on summary judgment, the Will Contest and Spousal Property Petition is continued to July 27, 2009 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Comm. Sue Kaplan, CSR: LaVena Ward #7077, Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha, Bailiff: Deputy Gomez.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer Entire Case To Probate Department On Ground That Civil Deparment Lacks Jurisdiction; Request For Judicial Notice With Exhibits
Off Calendar Apr-23-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES- ATTY. NICHOLAS J. BOOS OF THE SEDGWICK LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 627-1454 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. RICHARD A. CANATELLA OF THE LAW OFFICES OF COTTER & DEL CARLO, TEL. NO. 584-5446 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
ATTY. VICTORIA TRAN SOOD OF THE TRAN SOOD LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. (408) 625-7963 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT AND MOVING PARTY.

RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANT LILLIAN NAJARRO'S Motion To Transfer Entire Case To Probate Department On Ground That Civil Deparment Lacks Jurisdiction IS GRANTED IN ORDER TO ALLOW THE PROBATE DEPARTMENT TO DETERMINE WHETHER IT WILL ASSERT PRIMARY JURISDICTION OVER THE SUBJECT MATTER OF THE INSTANT INTERPLEADER ACTION AND THE PARTIES THERETO. CASE IS TRANSFERRED TO DEPARTMENT 2 0 4 FOR A STATUS HEARING ON MAY 13, 2009 AT 9:00 A.M. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =301/PJB
